Malaysia's delights source fame colourful, mouth-watering street food.
huge surprise Malaysian netizens find that Malaysia among countries featured Netflix's culinary series titled "Street Food Asia" which will launched April
two-minute trailer looked good showcased assortment street food their respective owners from different Southeast Asian countries such Thailand, Vietnam, India, Taiwan, South Korea, Indonesia, Japan, Philippines, Singapore there mention Malaysia all!
instant, local foodies immediately took Twitter convey their disapproval unhappiness over Netflix's decision dismiss Malaysian gastronomy completely, with some being extremely bitter about Singapore being chosen instead.
Street Food series which created producers Netflix's other smash-hit food show, Chef's Table, will "document blood, sweat tears" that into iconic street foods, pick street hawkers from nine "the world's most colorful cities" Singapore, Indonesia, Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines, India, Japan, Korea Taiwan featured show's first season.
According Netflix, Singaporean dishes that will find their into show include wonton noodles made late Tang Nam, chilli crab Seafood, chicken rice from Chicken Rice.
Also featured putu piring, steamed rice cake filled with palm sugar, made professional pastry chef Aisha Hashim, whose family owns Michelin Guide-approved Haig Road Putu Piring.
Singapore's hawker cuisine shares many similarities with Malaysia, prompting Malaysians many think their hawker heritage older richer feel about country's exclusion from lineup.
persistent protest among social media users over fact that Singapore chosen featured instead, reflection never-ending tension between Malaysia Singapore, whether about food not.
